blurb: WidgetKit enables app widgets and Apple Watch complications that may display sensitive information in prominent locations including the Lock Screen and Always On display. Developers apply Data Protection entitlements to widget extensions – using NSFileProtectionComplete or NSFileProtectionCompleteUnlessOpen – causing WidgetKit to render placeholder content instead of actual data when the device is locked. Users can independently configure Lock Screen widget visibility in Settings. On Apple Watch, Hide Sensitive Complications globally redacts complication data during Always On mode. Developers must implement the redacted(reason:) callback to supply appropriate placeholder views for privacy-sensitive widget content.
